title: 2. Personas and Customer Journey
body: > Audience and behavior from the `Consumer Journey — Sports World` document (client research), **adapted to the structure and the agreed flow of this spec** (questionnaire Q1–Q19, 6 Q4 goals, `welcome→…→briefing` phases, 11 page types in Part 3). Where the journey uses its own frameworks (8-phase funnel, "10 questions", Help Center, member portal, app), **what was agreed prevails**; from the journey we take the **archetypes**, the **4 entry doors** and the **actionable insights**.

title: 2.1 Personas
body:
The two CORE archetypes are **who** the user is (segmentation by life context); the **Q4 goal** they choose in the questionnaire is **what** they are looking for. The two axes coexist: the same archetype can choose different Q4 values.

**P1 — Family Wellbeing Manager ("Family CWO") · CORE Priority 1 · owner of the LTV (3x–4x the individual membership).**
35–50 years old · socioeconomic level AB/C+ · 1–2 children aged 4–12 · high-density areas (Del Valle, Polanco, Satélite, Interlomas, Pedregal). JTBD: *"have the club give me back time"* — train while the children are safe at FitKidz, without coordinating three locations.
- **Typical entry door (Rule 16/20):** local search focused on children ("gym con alberca para niños en [zona]", "natación niños cerca de [escuela]") → lands on a **club** or **amenity** page (FitKidz/pool).
- **Typical signals in the questionnaire:** Q4 = Lose weight · Improve my cardiovascular health · Recover from an injury; Q6 usually includes a pool; Q14 = "Me and my children" (→ Q14b).
- **What the Experiencia Ideal must achieve:** highlight FitKidz + the club's pool as resolved, and a brief that does not re-ask the family logistics. Absolute blocker: any sign of child insecurity. Slow decision (1–3 weeks; consults with the partner).

**P2 — Urban Hybrid Executive ("Third Spacer") · CORE Priority 2 · justifies the premium price.**
28–45 years old · hybrid/remote professional · lives or works near a legacy club (Antara, Reforma, Polanco, Santa Fe, Interlomas). JTBD: a **third space** to break up the day, train and shower in premium conditions.
- **Typical entry door:** hyperlocal or amenity-based search ("gym con vapor en Polanco", "club con coworking") → lands on a **club** or **amenity** page.
- **Typical signals in the questionnaire:** Q4 = Improve my athletic performance · Increase muscle mass · Improve my body aesthetics; Q7 early or post-work.
- **What the Experiencia Ideal must achieve:** fast confirmation (voice agent/WhatsApp), emphasis on amenities (steam/sauna/coworking) and multi-club. Faster decision; reviews 5–15 club ratings before deciding.

**P3 — Asesor (secondary, internal).** Receives the brief with flags and converts the visit without re-asking what has already been answered (Appendix G).
**P4 — Voice agent / BES (secondary, system).** Available 24/7, schedules and transfers to a human.

title: 2.2 Customer Journey — the Experiencia Ideal flow (from Google to the guided visit)
body:
The journey in this spec is the **agreed on-site flow**, not the industry's 8-phase funnel. Both archetypes go through the **same phases**; the divergences are tactical (what is emphasized).

```mermaid
journey
 title Experiencia Ideal — from search to guided visit
 section Discover (SEO · 4 doors)
 Local search on Google: 3: CWO, Executive
 Lands on hub/club/amenity (pre-fill Rule 20): 4: CWO, Executive
 section Qualify (Experiencia Ideal)
 Non-blocking invitation to the questionnaire (1x session): 3: CWO, Executive
 Answers adaptive Q1-Q19: 3: CWO, Executive
 Receives personalized recommendation: 5: CWO, Executive
 section Convert
 Leaves contact details: 4: CWO, Executive
 Schedules the visit (real-time confirmation): 5: CWO, Executive
 section Close (human)
 Asesor receives the brief and attends: 5: Asesor
 Voice agent confirms the appointment: 5: BES
```

The exact technical phases (welcome · questionnaire · loading · result · contact_capture · schedule · briefing · error) and all the branches are in **§3** and **§4**.

title: 2.3 Consumer journey insights that inform the design
body:
Each research insight connects to an **already agreed** rule/section (it does not introduce a new framework):

| Journey insight | Where it is addressed in the spec |
| --- | --- |
| The 4 entry doors reveal intent | Pre-fill by landing (Rule 16/20); the "lose weight" SEO hub is the goal door |
| Non-blocking invitation, 1×/session, persistent as a button | Questionnaire invocation behavior (Rule 27) |
| The review-check of the specific club decides the conversion | Club page (Part 3) with real photos, schedules, classes, reviews and amenities; Club Ideal (Rule 42) |
| Asesor consistency across 49 clubs + fast confirmation | Asesor brief (Appendix G) + real-time scheduling (`schedule` phase, client API) |
| Silent plateau (weeks 4–6) and return after absence = higher churn | **Out of scope for the site** (post-sale retention/CRM); noted as a dependency, not designed here |
| Benchmarks: NPS 47.3 · retention 66.4% · 50% churn at 6 months (industry) | Context for §10; the site impacts **acquisition**, not post-sale retention |

> **Precedence.** The journey describes "10 questions, 1 minute" and artifacts from another workstream (Help Center, app, member portal). What was agreed prevails: **official 15+6 questionnaire (Rule 18)**; Help Center out of scope (Rule 37 — covered by BES); app/portal are separate workstreams. The completion-time goal is measured against the official instrument (abandonment risk, §10.3).

---
